mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to rate changes over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which compounds into higher corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample R-squared above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion phase reveals supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression that trigger immediate negative market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 5-day returns, with mean forward returns exceeding 0.1% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate normalization that improves equity risk premiums, generating positive forward returns when monetary policy outpaces price growth.
failure_condition: The real rate spread shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns, with rank correlation consistently below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate wage expenditure and consumer spending capacity, mechanically depressing corporate earnings and yielding negative equity returns over three months.
failure_condition: Payroll declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with the signal producing positive alpha in 55% of historical observation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 3.0% confirms robust economic momentum that immediately boosts equity risk appetite and drives positive S&P 500 returns within the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: GDP releases above the 3.0% threshold do not generate statistically significant positive 5-day returns, with mean returns indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial output and rising unemployment during a recessionary regime signals severe demand destruction that forces immediate equity deleveraging and negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The supply-labor divergence during recessions fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with the signal showing positive predictive accuracy in over 50% of cases.
